definition:
1. gray, grizzled, grisly , of wolves, of iron, of the sea, (Iliad by Homer) 
2. mostly of hair, gray  or hoary  from age, (Homer); πολιοί gray-haired men , (Odyssey by Homer), etc.:—;absolute, αἱ πολιαί (i.e. τρίχες); (Pindar); ἅμα ταῖς πολιαῖς κατιούσαις as the gray hairs  come down (i.e. from the temples to the beard), (Aristophanes Comicus); π. δάκρυον ἐμβαλών an old man's  tear, (Euripides) 
3. metaphorically hoary, venerable , (Euripides) 
4. like λευκός, bright, clear, serene , (Hesiod) (ML)
